steps: Install the WebBLE Safari Web Extension from the App Store on the target iPhone and enable it under Settings → Apps → Safari → Extensions → WebBLE with 'Always Allow on Every Website' granted.
Drop a single <script> tag into your HTML that loads the CDN polyfill — no build step required.
Invoke navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice() from a user gesture (click/tap). Values stream via characteristicvaluechanged events once notifications are started.
Serve the page over HTTPS — Web Bluetooth requires a secure context.
Install WebBLE Safari Web Extension on the iPhone and enable it on every website.
Install the polyfill and React bindings:  npm install @ios-web-bluetooth/core @ios-web-bluetooth/react
Import '@ios-web-bluetooth/core' once at the entry (main.tsx) so navigator.bluetooth is installed before any component renders.
Use the useBluetoothDevice hook to drive a click-triggered requestDevice and stream characteristic values into component state.
Install WebBLE Safari Web Extension on the iPhone and enable it on every website.
Install the polyfill: npm install @ios-web-bluetooth/core
Import '@ios-web-bluetooth/core' once in main.ts before createApp() so navigator.bluetooth mounts before any component renders.
Invoke navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice() from a @click handler — it must run under user activation.
Install WebBLE Safari Web Extension on the iPhone and enable it on every website.
Install the polyfill: npm install @ios-web-bluetooth/core
Import '@ios-web-bluetooth/core' once in src/main.ts before mounting the root component so navigator.bluetooth is present on first render.
Call navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice() from an on:click handler to satisfy the user-activation requirement.
Install WebBLE Safari Web Extension on the iPhone and enable it on every website.
Install the polyfill and W3C types:  npm install @ios-web-bluetooth/core  && npm install --save-dev @types/web-bluetooth
Import '@ios-web-bluetooth/core' once in src/main.ts before bootstrapApplication() so navigator.bluetooth mounts before any zone/component runs.
Invoke navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice() from a (click) handler; run value updates through NgZone if you touch change detection.
Install WebBLE Safari Web Extension on the iPhone and enable it on every website.
Install the polyfill and React bindings:  npm install @ios-web-bluetooth/core @ios-web-bluetooth/react
Gate the polyfill to the browser by dynamic-importing '@ios-web-bluetooth/core' inside a 'use client' boundary (never at module scope — it would run during SSR).
Drive requestDevice() from a click handler in a client component; stream characteristic values into React state.
